channel.title: Yury's Reading List
channel.description: Articles, posts, and blogs curated by Yury

item.title: The Pulse: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ as a weird new trend
item.description: … which will probably be the shortest-lived trend because it’s so wasteful. Also: coding AI agent subsidies could be ending, Cal.com going closed source and blaming it on AI, and more.

item.title: Using Claude Code: session management and 1M context
item.description: Anthropic on managing Claude Code sessions with the new 1M context window. Covers session strategies, when to compact vs start fresh, subagent usage, and the new /usage command. Lots of variance in how users manage sessions.

item.title: Mechanical sympathy
item.description: On the connection between good engineering and good product design. Truly good engineers understand the contract between creator and user. The concept of affordances, mechanical sympathy, and why understanding how your system is used matters as much as how it works.

item.title: The impact of AI on software engineers in 2026: key trends
item.description: Our AI tooling survey finds concerns about mounting AI costs, more engineers hitting usage limits, and AI tools having uneven effects upon different types of engineers

item.title: Inside Meta's Home Grown AI Analytics Agent
item.description: Meta built an internal AI analytics agent that autonomously runs SQL queries, diagnoses metric drops, and traces root causes. Started as a hack by one data scientist, grew into a company-wide tool. The agent uses query history for context and can handle routine data analysis tasks that data scientists get asked repeatedly.

item.title: DHH’s new way of writing code
item.description: David Heinemeier Hansson shares why he shifted to an agent-first AI workflow, and what it means for how software is built and who builds it.

item.title: Cycles of disruption in the tech industry: with software pioneers Kent Beck & Martin Fowler
item.description: Parallels between technology shifts in the past decades and what we’re seeing with AI. Also: ways to avoid burnout when working with AI agents, TDD back in style, and more.

item.title: The Pulse: Industry leaders return to coding with AI
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg and Garry Tan join the trend of C-level folks jumping back into coding with AI. Also: a bad week for Claude Code and GitHub, and more

item.title: Scaling Uber with Thuan Pham (Uber’s first CTO)
item.description: Thuan Pham (Uber's first CTO) on scaling Uber from constant outages to global infrastructure, the shift to microservices and platform teams, and how AI is reshaping engineering.

item.title: What is inference engineering? Deepdive
item.description: Many engineers use inference daily, but inference engineering is a bit obscure – and an area rich with interesting challenges. Philip Kiely, author of the new book, “Inference Engineering,” explains

item.title: The Pulse: is GitHub still best for AI-native development?
item.description: Poor availability has dogged GitHub for months and raises questions about its status and focus. Plus, Microsoft promises Windows will not be “Microslop”, a massive LLM supply chain attack, and more

item.title: “How to be a 10x engineer” – interview with a standout dev
item.description: An interview with an engineer with no public GitHub contributions, setting clear boundaries – and yet not having needed to apply for positions when searching for a job, because referrals found them

item.title: The Pulse: Is the FDE role becoming less desirable?
item.description: Also: AI-agent generated pull requests cause headaches for large open source projects, OpenAI acquires the creator of uv, a sudden Cursor price hike annoys some enterprise customers, and more

item.title: Building WhatsApp with Jean Lee
item.description: Jean Lee, engineer #19 at WhatsApp, on scaling the app with a tiny team, the Facebook acquisition, and what it reveals about the future of engineering.

item.title: Are AI agents actually slowing us down?
item.description: As more software engineers use AI agents daily, there’s also more sloppy software, outages, quality issues, and even a slowdown in shipping velocity. What’s happening, and how do we solve it?

item.title: The Pulse: What will the Staff Engineer role look like in 2027 and beyond?
item.description: Also: new trend of token costs becoming a worry for CTOs, 10% cuts at Atlassian, and more.

item.title: From IDEs to AI Agents with Steve Yegge
item.description: Steve Yegge on how AI is reshaping software engineering, the rise of “vibe coding,” and why developers must adapt to a rapidly changing craft.

item.title: How Uber uses AI for development: inside look
item.description: How Uber built Minion, Shepherd, uReview, and other internal agentic AI tools. Also, new challenges in rolling out AI tools, like more platform investment and growing concern about token costs

item.title: The Pulse: AWS region knocked offline by drone attack in historic first
item.description: Also: 40% job cuts at Block “not due to AI”, Antigravity bans reinforce Google’s disdain for paying customers, and more

item.title: Building Claude Code with Boris Cherny
item.description: Claude Code creator Boris Cherny on building AI-powered coding tools, parallel agents, and how the engineer's role is evolving in an AI-first world.

item.title: AI Tooling for Software Engineers in 2026
item.description: Claude Code dominates tool usage, leaders are more positive about AI than engineers, staff+ engineers are the biggest users of AI agents, and more. Exclusive data and analysis from 900+ respondents

item.title: The Pulse: Cloudflare rewrites Next.js as AI rewrites commercial open source
item.description: An engineer at Cloudflare rewrote most of Vercel’s Next.js in one week with AI agents. It looks like a sign of how AI will disrupt existing moats and business models. Analysis

item.title: Mitchell Hashimoto’s new way of writing code
item.description: Mitchell Hashimoto on building HashiCorp, navigating the cloud giants, and how AI agents have transformed his day-to-day engineering workflow.

item.title: The Future of Software Engineering with AI: Six Predictions
item.description: Notes from The Pragmatic Summit and ‘The Future of Software Development’ workshop

item.title: The Pulse #162: Even fewer middle managers and more flexible teams?
item.description: Also: Anthropic bans third-party devs while Codex embraces them, Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI, a lot more software gets created with AI, and more
